message:
Git protocol v2 support: ls-refs and fetch commands

Adds v2 to smart-http.ts alongside the existing v1 implementation.
Modern Git CLIs default to v2; this rounds out the protocol coverage.

V2 negotiation:
- discoverRefs sends `Git-Protocol: version=2` header by default
- If the server response begins with `version 2\n`, parse capabilities
  and follow up with a v2 ls-refs POST
- Otherwise fall back to v1 ref-advertisement parsing
- protocolVersion: "v1" forces the old path (skips the v2 header entirely)
- protocolVersion: "v2" forces v2 (no fallback)

V2 ls-refs:
  POST /git-upload-pack with body:
    command=ls-refs\n agent=…\n object-format=sha1\n
    <delim> peel\n symrefs\n ref-prefix HEAD\n ref-prefix refs/heads/\n …
    <flush>
  Response: <sha> <ref> [symref-target:<target>] [peeled:<sha>]\n …

V2 fetch:
  POST /git-upload-pack with body:
    command=fetch\n agent=…\n object-format=sha1\n
    <delim> ofs-delta\n want <sha>\n … done\n
    <flush>
  Response: packfile\n + sideband-multiplexed pack data + flush
  (sideband-all is implicit in v2; no need to opt in via capability)

V2 demux differs from v1: v1 has a NAK/ACK preamble before pack data,
v2 has explicit section headers ("packfile\n"). demuxSidebandV2 handles
this and skips other section headers (acknowledgments, shallow-info)
that aren't relevant for a basic fetch.

5 new tests:
- auto-detects v2 from info/refs and follows up with ls-refs
- falls back to v1 when server doesn't advertise v2
- protocolVersion: "v1" skips v2 negotiation
- v2 fetch body shape + Git-Protocol header
- v2 sideband channel separation (progress / errors)

Existing artifacts-fetch and full-pipeline tests still pass — they use
v1 by default which is unchanged.

97 tests in edge-compute-pool (was 92); 142 across all packages (was 137).

files.patch:
@@ -236,6 +236,7 @@ Earlier rounds left the toolkit *positioned* to extend Artifacts but with no act
 - [x] **`pkt-line.ts`** — Git's framing protocol (length-prefixed packets, flush/delim markers, sideband demultiplexing). 12 tests.
 - [x] **`packfile-reader.ts`** — Pack v2 parser. Verifies SHA-1 trailer; parses type+size headers; uses `WasmEngine.zlibInflateTracked` and `deltaApply` for the heavy work; resolves both ref-delta and ofs-delta. Decoupled from any storage layer — exposes `onObject` callback for streaming or returns the unpacked map. 7 tests including a synthetic pack-builder fixture and corruption-detection.
 - [x] **`smart-http.ts`** — Git smart HTTP v1 client. `discoverRefs` (GET `/info/refs?service=git-upload-pack`, parse first-line capabilities + ref advertisement). `fetchPack` (POST `/git-upload-pack` with want/done pkt-lines, sideband-64k response demux). 9 tests with hand-crafted pkt-line responses and Authorization header verification.
+- [x] **Git protocol v2 support** — added to `smart-http.ts` after v1 was working. `discoverRefs` auto-negotiates: sends `Git-Protocol: version=2` header, detects v2 capability advertisement, follows up with a v2 `ls-refs` POST. `fetchPack` accepts `protocolVersion: "v2"` to issue a `command=fetch` POST instead of v1 want/done. V2 sideband demux handles the `packfile` section header that v1 doesn't have. 5 new tests covering: auto-detection of v2 from info/refs, fallback to v1 when v2 not advertised, explicit `protocolVersion: "v1"` skips v2 negotiation, v2 fetch body shape + Git-Protocol header, sideband channel separation in v2 fetch responses.
 - [x] **`fetchArtifactsCommit` coordinator** — Orchestrates fetch → unpack → re-deflate → write-to-R2. Each unpacked object is recompressed as a standard loose git object (`zlib(<type> <size>\0<content>)`) and written at `${repoPath}/loose/${sha}` — exactly the layout the existing pool actions read from. 4 integration tests including the killer one: an in-memory Artifacts-shaped server is stood up, a commit closure is fetched + staged, and then the existing `parse-commits` and `read-blobs` actions are dispatched against the staged objects, returning the same shape they would for any other R2-backed repo.
 
 The end-to-end test is the actual proof. Without it, "works with Artifacts" was wishful thinking. With it, the only thing standing between this toolkit and a real Artifacts beta repo is a beta access token.

@@ -163,7 +163,7 @@ The integration test (`test/artifacts-fetch.integration.test.ts`) stands up an i
 - Unpack is **not** fan-out-able — delta chains require ordering within a packfile.
 - One coordinator can saturate ~20 slots; beyond that you're better off sharding work across multiple coordinators.
 - The package depends on `@gitmode/wasm-git` for SHA-1, zlib, delta, packfile primitives. Cross-package WASM imports under `vitest-pool-workers` require `test.deps.optimizer.ssr.include: ["@gitmode/wasm-git"]` in the consumer's `vitest.config.ts` (already wired up in this package's own tests).
-- Smart-HTTP client targets Git protocol v1 (which Artifacts also supports). Protocol v2 (`Git-Protocol: version=2`) would need a separate ls-refs / fetch command implementation and is not shipped.
+- Smart-HTTP client supports both Git protocol v1 and v2 (`Git-Protocol: version=2`). `discoverRefs` auto-negotiates: it sends the v2 header by default, follows up with a v2 ls-refs POST when the server advertises `version 2`, and falls back to v1 parsing otherwise. `fetchPack` defaults to v1 (works with all servers) but accepts `protocolVersion: "v2"` to use v2 fetch instead.
 
 ## Status / extraction
